report_name: Two U.S. Senior Care Providers Disclose Data Breaches by Sinobi and Worldleaks Ransomware Gangs

threat_actors.name: Sinobi
threat_actors.description: Ransomware group responsible for the attack on Windward Life Care, employing double-extortion tactics by exfiltrating 25GB of data before encryption.

threat_actors.name: Worldleaks
threat_actors.description: Threat group responsible for the ransomware attack on Legend Senior Living, leaking stolen data on its dark web site after ransom demands were not met.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware was deployed by both Sinobi and Worldleaks to encrypt files across the victims' networks, causing operational disruption and locking the organizations out of their systems.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Both attacker groups exfiltrated large volumes of sensitive data, including PII and PHI, to their own infrastructure before deploying ransomware. The Sinobi group exfiltrated 25 gigabytes of data.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The use of tools like Mimikatz was suggested as a likely method for credential theft during the attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The use of tools like PsExec was suggested as a likely method for lateral movement within the compromised networks.

malware.name: Sinobi Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Sinobi group to encrypt files at Windward Life Care as part of a double-extortion attack.

malware.name: Worldleaks Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Worldleaks group to encrypt systems at Legend Senior Living after exfiltrating sensitive data.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as a likely tool used by the attackers for credential theft.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a likely tool used for lateral movement within the victim networks.

identities.name: Windward Life Care
identities.description: A California-based senior care provider that suffered a data breach from a ransomware attack by the Sinobi group in late 2025.

identities.name: Legend Senior Living
identities.description: A Kansas-based senior care provider that suffered a data breach from a ransomware attack by the Worldleaks group in mid-2025, affecting at least 5,006 residents in Texas.

infrastructure.name: Sinobi Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Sinobi group to leak 25GB of data stolen from Windward Life Care.

infrastructure.name: Worldleaks Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Worldleaks group to publish data stolen from Legend Senior Living.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a single network connection sending an unusually large amount of data (over 1GB), which could indicate data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/two-senior-care-providers-affected-by-ransomware-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1048
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Initiated: true
    SentBytes|gt: 1000000000
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large, unexpected data transfers leaving the network, such as the 25GB exfiltrated by the Sinobi group.

indicators.name: Net Use Command for Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: Net Use to Map Remote Admin Share
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-9e8f7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net.exe' to map a remote administrative share (C$), often used for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/two-senior-care-providers-affected-by-ransomware-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'use'
      - '\\'
      - 'C$'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'net use' command to access administrative shares on remote systems, a common lateral movement technique.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a tool commonly used for credential dumping.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/two-senior-care-providers-affected-by-ransomware-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    - Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
    - OriginalFileName: 'mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Mimikatz tool, which was suggested as a likely method for credential theft in these attacks.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec Service Execution
id: 9e8f7d6c-5b4a-4d2c-1b0a-8d7c6b5a4f3e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the PsExec service executable (PSEXESVC.exe) on a remote system, which indicates lateral movement via PsExec.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/two-senior-care-providers-affected-by-ransomware-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1569.002
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\PSEXESVC.exe'
    OriginalFileName: 'psexesvc.exe'
  condition: 1 of selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of PsExec for lateral movement, a technique suggested as likely in the report.

relationships.source_name: Net Use Command for Lateral Movement
